subject: grammar
title: Ability: can, could, be able to, manage to
instruction: Underline (choose) the most suitable words. Sometimes both options are possible.

questions.text: He's a concert pianist and he ___ to play all Beethoven's sonatas.
When I lived in a small town I ___ walk almost everywhere, but now I live in the capital city I need a car.
They worked all night and ___ to finish the report just in time.
The protestors ___ persuade the president to change the law.
Next year she ___ join the club, but she's not old enough yet.
In my country it ___ get very cold in the winter.
I was nearly late as the bus didn't come, but luckily I ___ to get a taxi.
I hope that I ___ sightseeing when I'm in New York next week on business, but I've got a busy schedule.

options.A: will be able to / will manage to
options.B: will manage to / will be able to

questions.note: Image shows both forms as individual options but usually they are separate A/B. I will use A and B separately.

questions.text: I hope that I ___ some sightseeing when I'm in New York next week on business, but I've got a busy schedule.
She didn't get good enough grades to go to her first choice of university but she ___ get a place at another one.
